title: The Intensifying Geopolitics of Southeast Asia’s Military Modernization
author: James Guild
text:
The Rafale fighter jet, which is manufactured by the French aerospace firm Dassault.
Credit: Wikimedia Commons/Tim Felce (Airwolfhound)
Many Southeast Asian countries have stepped up military modernization efforts in recent years. This is a function of sharpening geopolitical tensions and increased risk, especially for countries like Indonesia and the Philippines that are embroiled in territorial disputes with China. The erosion of the U.S. security umbrella, and unpredictable American policy more generally, is incentivizing regional governments to increase defense spending.There is a greater sense of urgency to acquire warships, aircraft, and missile systems than there was a decade ago. This has been an ongoing process, but several key developments in recent months are worth mentioning, as they underscore how defense modernization in Southeast Asia is not just a matter of buying the most effective equipment at the best price but is also influenced by wider geopolitical forces.[...]
